title: Source lineage for ash3725
summary: The repository XML is a normalization-only descendant of the U-M/CME encoding embedded in a complete raw package. That encoding was produced from page images of Furnivall's 1901-1902 printed edition. The edition chiefly transcribes Dd.4.24 and fills its gaps and ending from Egerton 2726, while the Clerk text passes through Wright's 1867 print and is recollated to Dd. Tyrwhitt, Skeat, Arundel 248, Ellesmere, and Gg.4.27 have narrower editorial, textual, or visual roles.
review_notes: Keep abstract work, manuscript witnesses, scholarly editions, print carrier, page images, encoding, preservation package, and repository XML distinct.
The U-M raw package is represented by a catalog_description schema adapter because the current entity enum lacks a digital-package type.
The two scoped version_of relations are schema adapters for proven Hooper visual adaptation only; neither relation asserts textual ancestry or facsimile identity.
The carrier includes twenty-three Ellesmere and seven Gg issued surfaces. Advertised Gg Pardoner and Monk surfaces are absent and remain a carrier-state question.
Furnivall explicitly withdrew the Laud K 50 hypothesis for the later Egerton f. 271 Parson ending; Laud is not modeled as a source.
U-M's 787 logical IIIF canvases and the raw package's 786 unique TIFF files are retained as different presentation and file-inventory facts.
Public-domain historical content does not transfer automatically to modern photography, provider metadata, OCR/TEI, or repository serialization.

entities.label: Furnivall's Dd.4.24 and Egerton 2726 Canterbury Tales edition, 1901-1902
alternate_labels: The Cambridge MS. Dd. 4. 24. of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales, Completed by the Egerton MS. 2726 (the Haistwell MS)
entities.description: Two-part Chaucer Society diplomatic edition of The Canterbury Tales, chiefly from CUL Dd.4.24 and completed from BL Egerton 2726, with separately identified textual and visual appendices.

bibliographic.citation: Geoffrey Chaucer, The Cambridge MS. Dd. 4. 24. of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales, Completed by the Egerton MS. 2726 (the Haistwell MS), ed. Frederick J. Furnivall, Chaucer Society First Series 95-96 (London, 1901-1902).
bibliographic.title: The Cambridge MS. Dd. 4. 24. of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales, Completed by the Egerton MS. 2726 (the Haistwell MS)
bibliographic.publisher: Kegan Paul, Trench, Trübner & Co. for the Chaucer Society

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Dd.4.24
entities.description: Early fifteenth-century English paper Canterbury Tales witness. The text occupies ff.6r-205v and ends imperfectly at Canon's Yeoman's Tale VIII.855; missing leaves create additional internal lacunae. A possible scribal name, Wytton, remains uncertain.

date_statements.label: production
date_statements.value: first quarter of the fifteenth century

notes: Thirty-six conservation replacement leaves are recorded; most are not digitized.

entities.label: Cambridge Digital Library Dd.4.24 IIIF presentation
entities.description: Complete provider-published 377-canvas digital presentation of MS Dd.4.24.
entities.reproduction_format: 377-canvas IIIF Presentation 2 object with downloadable JPEG derivatives

entities.label: British Library Egerton MS 2726 (Haistwell Manuscript)
entities.description: English Canterbury Tales manuscript of 271 folios, written by two scribes in 1425-1449. It includes an eighteenth-century paper Gamelyn copy at ff.56r-63v and an imperfect Parson ending on an eighteenth-century tipped leaf f.271.

entities.label: The Clerk's Tale printed from MS Dd.4.24, 1867
entities.description: Forty-two-page diplomatic print of the Clerk's Prologue and Tale from Cambridge MS Dd.4.24, attributed by Furnivall to William Aldis Wright.

bibliographic.citation: Geoffrey Chaucer, The Clerk's Tale: Printed from MS. Dd. 4. 24 in the University Library, Cambridge (1867).
bibliographic.title: The Clerk's Tale: Printed from MS. Dd. 4. 24 in the University Library, Cambridge

entities.label: Harvard/Google scan of The Clerk's Tale, 1867
entities.description: Complete 52-page scan carrier, including covers, title matter, and the forty-two printed text pages.

entities.label: Thomas Tyrwhitt's Canterbury Tales, 1775-1778
entities.description: First-issue five-volume edition of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales with introductory and explanatory apparatus.

bibliographic.citation: Geoffrey Chaucer, The Canterbury Tales of Chaucer, ed. Thomas Tyrwhitt, 5 vols. (London, 1775-1778).
bibliographic.title: The Canterbury Tales of Chaucer

entities.label: Internet Archive scan of Tyrwhitt volume III
entities.description: Complete 342-page digital scan of volume III of the first-issue Tyrwhitt edition.
entities.reproduction_format: text PDF with OCR and scan-structure derivatives

entities.label: Skeat's Complete Works of Geoffrey Chaucer, volume IV, 1894
entities.description: Oxford Clarendon Press edition of The Canterbury Tales constituting volume IV of Walter W. Skeat's Complete Works of Geoffrey Chaucer.

bibliographic.citation: Geoffrey Chaucer, The Complete Works of Geoffrey Chaucer, vol. 4, The Canterbury Tales, ed. Walter W. Skeat (Oxford: Clarendon Press, 1894).
bibliographic.title: The Complete Works of Geoffrey Chaucer, Volume IV: The Canterbury Tales

entities.label: British Library Arundel MS 248
entities.description: English parchment musical and devotional miscellany of 201 folios; f.154 contains the Latin Angelus ad virginem and Middle English Gabriel fram evene king with musical notation.

date_statements.label: production
date_statements.value: mid-thirteenth century; legacy BL record broadly catalogs fourteenth-fifteenth century contents

entities.label: Huntington Library MS EL 26 C 9 (Ellesmere Chaucer)
entities.description: Deluxe illuminated parchment manuscript of The Canterbury Tales produced in England c.1400-1410, with twenty-two pilgrim miniatures and a likely portrait of Chaucer.

physical_description.extent: 232 main-text folios plus preliminary and final leaves

entities.label: Huntington Ellesmere Chaucer IIIF presentation

entities.reproduction_format: 480-canvas IIIF Presentation 2 object with JPEG derivatives

entities.label: Cambridge University Library MS Gg.4.27(1)
entities.description: Large illustrated Chaucer anthology compiled in East Anglia, probably near Cambridge, primarily on parchment. The Canterbury Tales occupies ff.132r-443v; many illustrated leaves were removed and some replacement leaves were supplied by Joseph Holland around 1600.

rights.component: repository XML, OCR text, and markup as checked in
rights.basis: The CME root Unlicense is worded for software/source or binary code and does not clearly grant a license for corpus XML, OCR, or markup. Public-domain status of the medieval text and historical edition does not by itself settle the separate digital-encoding layer.

rights.component: U-M electronic text and encoding
rights.basis: The current item is labeled Public Domain and No Copyright-United States.
notes: The 2006 XML header preserves older generic restriction language; both statements are recorded. NoC-US is a rights statement, not an open license.

rights.component: U-M page-image sequence
rights.basis: The current U-M item applies its Public Domain and No Copyright-United States designation to the digital item and linked pages.

rights.component: raw ZIP aggregation and its files
rights.jurisdiction: United States; U-M and Internet Archive service context
rights.basis: The historical content is no-known-copyright under the current U-M item statement, but no separate open license was verified for every technical/log/metadata file or the package aggregation.

rights.component: Internet Archive API metadata
rights.basis: No file-specific open license for the full metadata response was verified; factual fields and response serialization remain distinct.

rights.component: published medieval text, Furnivall editorial matter, Hooper woodcuts, appendices, and typographical arrangement
rights.basis: Publication in 1901-1902 is before the United States pre-1931 cutoff. Furnivall died in 1910 and Hooper in 1912, so their published contributions exceed life-plus-70; the typographical arrangement also exceeds the UK term.

rights.component: medieval manuscript text and historical artifact
rights.basis: The medieval text and manuscript artifact predate modern copyright. Custody, access rules, catalog text, and digital reproductions are separately assessed.

rights.component: downloadable CUDL images and object metadata
rights.basis: The IIIF attribution identifies downloadable images as CC BY-NC 4.0. The provider metadata text separately states CC BY-NC-ND 4.0, while the manifest attribution also contains all-rights-reserved wording for zoom imagery.
notes: Apply the correct license to the correct component; do not flatten image and metadata permissions.
The CC BY-NC 4.0 license applies to downloadable images. CUDL metadata is separately CC BY-NC-ND 4.0, and zoom-image/all-rights wording is not flattened into either component license.

rights.component: medieval manuscript content only
rights.basis: The medieval manuscript content is public domain. Current British Library catalog text and any future supplied photography are assessed separately in dedicated component rights records.

rights.component: published 1867 text and editorial presentation
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1867, before the United States pre-1931 cutoff; William Aldis Wright died in 1914, beyond life-plus-70.

rights.component: Google-scanned PDF carrier of the public-domain edition
rights.basis: The scanned edition is public domain. No separate open license for all scan-file layers was verified; embedded Google and Internet Archive service terms remain separate from copyright in the edition.
notes: The underlying historical edition is public domain; this record conservatively leaves possible scan-file or derivative-layer rights unresolved.

rights.component: published text and editorial apparatus
rights.basis: The edition was published in 1775-1778 and is out of copyright.

rights.component: PDF, OCR, metadata, and scandata derivatives
rights.basis: The underlying edition is public domain. No one file-specific open license covering every cached derivative was verified.
notes: The underlying historical edition is public domain; this record conservatively leaves possible scan-file or derivative-layer rights unresolved.

rights.component: published 1894 text and editorial apparatus
rights.basis: Publication in 1894 is before the United States pre-1931 cutoff; Skeat died in 1912, beyond life-plus-70.

rights.component: BSB digital object and image derivatives
rights.basis: The exact IIIF manifest carries No Copyright - Non-Commercial Use Only 1.0.
notes: NoC-NC is a rights statement and reuse constraint, not a Creative Commons license.

rights.component: medieval manuscript content only
rights.basis: The medieval manuscript content is public domain. Current British Library catalog text and any future supplied photography are assessed separately in dedicated component rights records.

rights.component: medieval manuscript content
rights.basis: The medieval manuscript and text predate modern copyright; digital file rights and institutional policy are separate.

rights.component: Huntington IIIF manifest and image derivatives
rights.basis: Huntington says it does not require permission or charge fees for public-domain material or material whose copyright it does not own, asks for citation, and places copyright determination on the researcher. The exact IIIF manifest does not provide a machine-readable open license for the image files.

rights.component: medieval and early modern manuscript content
rights.basis: The manuscript's medieval and early modern contents predate modern copyright. Catalog metadata and digital images are separately licensed.

rights.component: downloadable CUDL images and object metadata
rights.basis: Downloadable images are stated as CC BY-NC 4.0 and metadata as CC BY-NC-ND 4.0; zoom-image/all-rights-reserved wording must not be flattened into the download license.
notes: The CC BY-NC 4.0 license applies to downloadable images. CUDL metadata is separately CC BY-NC-ND 4.0, and zoom-image/all-rights wording is not flattened into either component license.

rights.component: CUDL catalog metadata
rights.basis: Cambridge Digital Library separately licenses object metadata under CC BY-NC-ND 4.0.

rights.component: CUDL catalog metadata
rights.basis: Cambridge Digital Library separately licenses object metadata under CC BY-NC-ND 4.0.

rights.component: British Library catalog text
rights.basis: The current BL catalog footer licenses catalog text under CC BY 4.0; that license does not extend to manuscript images.

rights.component: Any newly supplied British Library photography
rights.basis: Current Imaging Services terms require written permission before reproducing supplied images; no requested image payload has yet been received or licensed.

rights.component: British Library catalog text
rights.basis: The current BL catalog footer licenses catalog text under CC BY 4.0; that license does not extend to manuscript images.

rights.component: Any newly supplied British Library photography
rights.basis: Current Imaging Services terms require written permission before reproducing supplied images; no requested image payload has yet been received or licensed.

editorial_practices.description: Uses Dd.4.24 as the principal text and Egerton 2726 only where Dd is lacunose or has ended; exact XML source-switch notes preserve each transition.
The Clerk's Prologue and Tale were set from Wright's 1867 print and recollated against Dd.4.24.
Skeat supplies omitted-line Appendix 1, Arundel 248 supplies the Angelus appendix, and the exact visual suites contain twenty-three Ellesmere and seven Gg surfaces.
editorial_practices.scope: Printed pp. 679-688 and issued plate matter

evidence.citation: BL Egerton 2726 and Arundel 248 public-image and photography audit
evidence.summary: No complete public image set was verified for either manuscript. The BL offers manuscript photography through an online form; requests should give exact shelfmarks/folios and explicitly ask for color. Supplied-image reuse requires written permission under the current terms. physical manuscripts only; no local image payload
notes: The current price card lists £27.70 for a first TIFF and £7.25 for each subsequent TIFF in the relevant service tier; a full codex would therefore likely cost thousands of pounds before any additional charges. This is an estimate, not a quote.

evidence.citation: Visual audit of the exact U-M raw carrier front matter and appendices
evidence.locator: TIFF 9-11: Furnivall foreword; TIFF 16: appendix contents; TIFF 23: printed p.1; TIFF 700: printed p.678; TIFF 701-710: printed pp.679-688; TIFF 711-773: plate matter; TIFF 775 onward: Chaucer Society matter
evidence.summary: complete edition-defined representation with carrier boundaries
notes: The foreword colophon visibly reads 21 February 1903 despite the 1902 Part II title and 1901-1902 issue statement.

The source statements assign Dd as the principal witness, Egerton as supplement, Wright as Clerk typesetting intermediary, Tyrwhitt as paragraphing aid, Skeat as Appendix 1 source, and named manuscripts as appendix sources.
